records.stdout_tail:
: plasticity has component independent of eta(Yd)

  kappa=0 physical interpretation:
    Old (v3.7): plasticity creates r-dependent gc(r) via C14
    New (v4.7): plasticity appears as inter-galaxy gc variation (C15 scatter)
    kappa=0 means no spatial coupling within membrane,
    but allows different global f_p per galaxy.
    S_gal is an observational proxy for this f_p.

  Figure saved: D:\ドキュメント\エントロピー\新膜宇宙論\これまでの軌跡\パイソン\sparc_plasticity_results.png

======================================================================
Summary
======================================================================

  This analysis independently tests the plasticity region hypothesis
  (Miyaoka 2018, 16 clusters) at SPARC galaxy scale (175 galaxies).

  S_gal (galaxy-level plasticity indicator):
    f_gas:    gas fraction (plastic -> high)
    Yd:       mass-to-light ratio (plastic -> high)
    1-SBdisk: inverse surface brightness (plastic -> low SB)
    Ttype:    morphological type (plastic -> late type)

  Under kappa=0 (v4.7):
    Plasticity appears as inter-galaxy gc variation (C15 scatter 0.286 dex),
    NOT as radial gc(r) dependence (C14).
    S_gal is the observational proxy for f_p (plasticity fraction per galaxy).

  Miyaoka bridge:
    If S_plastic (cluster) and S_gal (galaxy) reflect the same physics
    (membrane elastic/plastic two-phase structure at different scales),
    a monotonic correspondence via morphological type is expected.

====================
T5: Independence test
======================================================================
  r(gc_deep, vflat^2/hR) = 0.814 (p=5.04e-37)
  r(gc_known, vflat^2/hR) = 0.684 (ref)
  NOTE: gc_deep = gobs^2/gbar is noisy estimate of gc_known
        No independent info (deep-MOND identity)

======================================================================
T6: Vobs-only fundamental limitation
======================================================================
  Deep-MOND inversion gc = gobs^2/gbar requires gbar.
  Without Vbar decomposition:
    (a) gc=a0 (MOND assumption) -> circular
    (b) gc free + gbar free -> 1 eq, 2 unknowns
    (c) inner Newtonian fit needs Yd
  => Not truly Vobs-only.

  T1 remains useful: MOND (gc=a0) vs membrane (gc=galaxy-dependent)
  discrimination test.

======================================================================
Summary
======================================================================
  T1 MOND: alpha=0.376+/-0.031, p(a=0)=7.68e-33
       -> MOND rejected. gc is galaxy-dependent.
  T3 gc_deep: r=0.904, scatter=0.188, alpha=0.674+/-0.049

  All S-2 approaches:
    A: Deep-MOND -> requires gbar (Vbar dependent)
    B: Shape -> r_t/v_t circularity
    C: Photometric -> Yd-alpha degeneracy

  Independent routes remaining:
    1. HSC weak lensing / cluster profiles
    2. Condition-14 epsilon threshold
    3. PROBES/MaNGA for precision vflat+hR


Figure saved.

Done.

======================================================================
Interpretation Guide
======================================================================

If all methods give alpha ~ 0.65-0.70:
  -> The 0.125 gap vs TA3 is due to g_c definition, not Y_d.

  -> Next step: read TA3 source code to understand its g_c definition.

If some method gives alpha ~ 0.54:
  -> That method matches TA3. The gap is explained by g_c definition choice.
  -> Document which definition is physically most appropriate.

If TA3 g_c values are systematically lower than v2:
  -> TA3 may account for adiabatic contraction or non-circular motions,
     which reduce g_c and push alpha toward 0.5.

records.stdout_tail:
: plasticity has component independent of eta(Yd)

  kappa=0 physical interpretation:
    Old (v3.7): plasticity creates r-dependent gc(r) via C14
    New (v4.7): plasticity appears as inter-galaxy gc variation (C15 scatter)
    kappa=0 means no spatial coupling within membrane,
    but allows different global f_p per galaxy.
    S_gal is an observational proxy for this f_p.

  Figure saved: D:\ドキュメント\エントロピー\新膜宇宙論\これまでの軌跡\パイソン\sparc_plasticity_results.png

======================================================================
Summary
======================================================================

  This analysis independently tests the plasticity region hypothesis
  (Miyaoka 2018, 16 clusters) at SPARC galaxy scale (175 galaxies).

  S_gal (galaxy-level plasticity indicator):
    f_gas:    gas fraction (plastic -> high)
    Yd:       mass-to-light ratio (plastic -> high)
    1-SBdisk: inverse surface brightness (plastic -> low SB)
    Ttype:    morphological type (plastic -> late type)

  Under kappa=0 (v4.7):
    Plasticity appears as inter-galaxy gc variation (C15 scatter 0.286 dex),
    NOT as radial gc(r) dependence (C14).
    S_gal is the observational proxy for f_p (plasticity fraction per galaxy).

  Miyaoka bridge:
    If S_plastic (cluster) and S_gal (galaxy) reflect the same physics
    (membrane elastic/plastic two-phase structure at different scales),
    a monotonic correspondence via morphological type is expected.

CONDITION 15 Summary

  Self-referential loop:
    gc = eta * sqrt(a0 * Sigma_dyn)   [geometric mean, alpha_dyn~0.59]
    Sigma_dyn = sqrt(2pi * G*Sigma_bar * gc)  [deep MOND]
    => gc = eta^(4/3) * a0^(2/3) * (2pi*G*Sigma_bar)^(1/3)

  Predictions:
    (C15a) alpha_bar = 1/3          observed: 0.136  (gap 0.197)
    (C15b) alpha_bar = a_dyn/(2-a_dyn) = 0.418  observed: 0.136  (gap 0.281)

  Deep-MOND identity:
    r = 0.713, scatter = 0.408 dex -> explains 54% of Sigma_dyn variance

  Variance decomposition:
    gc contributes 15%, Sigma_bar 30%
    => Self-reference is real but not dominant

  Implication:
    alpha_dyn~0.5 is partly self-referential (Sigma_dyn encodes gc).
    True baryonic exponent alpha_bar~0.14-0.22 << 1/3.
    Gap from 1/3: Yd variance + non-deep-MOND + disk geometry.

  2. SIS 1-halo is simplified (NFW would improve)
  3. Order-of-magnitude estimate, not precision
  4. Exact 2-halo measurement requires HSC Phase B decomposition

======================================================================
From g_obs = (g_bar + sqrt(g_bar^2 + 4*gc*g_bar))/2, solve for gc:
gc = (g_obs - g_bar)^2 / g_bar  [valid when g_obs > g_bar]

  Deep-MOND inversion gc = gobs^2/gbar requires gbar.

    (b) gc free + gbar free -> 1 eq, 2 unknowns
    (c) inner Newtonian fit needs Yd

    B: Shape -> r_t/v_t circularity
    C: Photometric -> Yd-alpha degeneracy

  -> Document which definition is physically most appropriate.

If TA3 g_c values are systematically lower than v2:
  -> TA3 may account for adiabatic contraction or non-circular motions,
     which reduce g_c and push alpha toward 0.5.

: plasticity has component independent of eta(Yd)

  kappa=0 physical interpretation:
    Old (v3.7): plasticity creates r-dependent gc(r) via C14
    New (v4.7): plasticity appears as inter-galaxy gc variation (C15 scatter)
    kappa=0 means no spatial coupling within membrane,
    but allows different global f_p per galaxy.
    S_gal is an observational proxy for this f_p.

  Figure saved: D:\ドキュメント\エントロピー\新膜宇宙論\これまでの軌跡\パイソン\sparc_plasticity_results.png

======================================================================
Summary
======================================================================

  This analysis independently tests the plasticity region hypothesis
  (Miyaoka 2018, 16 clusters) at SPARC galaxy scale (175 galaxies).

  S_gal (galaxy-level plasticity indicator):
    f_gas:    gas fraction (plastic -> high)
    Yd:       mass-to-light ratio (plastic -> high)
    1-SBdisk: inverse surface brightness (plastic -> low SB)
    Ttype:    morphological type (plastic -> late type)

  Under kappa=0 (v4.7):
    Plasticity appears as inter-galaxy gc variation (C15 scatter 0.286 dex),
    NOT as radial gc(r) dependence (C14).
    S_gal is the observational proxy for f_p (plasticity fraction per galaxy).

  Miyaoka bridge:
    If S_plastic (cluster) and S_gal (galaxy) reflect the same physics
    (membrane elastic/plastic two-phase structure at different scales),
    a monotonic correspondence via morphological type is expected.

  Self-referential loop:
    gc = eta * sqrt(a0 * Sigma_dyn)   [geometric mean, alpha_dyn~0.59]
    Sigma_dyn = sqrt(2pi * G*Sigma_bar * gc)  [deep MOND]
    => gc = eta^(4/3) * a0^(2/3) * (2pi*G*Sigma_bar)^(1/3)

  Predictions:
    (C15a) alpha_bar = 1/3          observed: 0.136  (gap 0.197)
    (C15b) alpha_bar = a_dyn/(2-a_dyn) = 0.418  observed: 0.136  (gap 0.281)

  Deep-MOND identity:

  Variance decomposition:
    gc contributes 15%, Sigma_bar 30%
    => Self-reference is real but not dominant

  Implication:
    alpha_dyn~0.5 is partly self-referential (Sigma_dyn encodes gc).
    True baryonic exponent alpha_bar~0.14-0.22 << 1/3.
    Gap from 1/3: Yd variance + non-deep-MOND + disk geometry.
